frameworks.name: cortical hierarchy / principal gradient of macroscale organization
frameworks.one_line: unimodal-to-transmodal axis organizing local versus distributed processing

frameworks.name: task-evoked activity vs functional connectivity dissociation
frameworks.one_line: contrasts localized BOLD activation with distributed task-state FC change

frameworks.name: intrinsic timescale framework
frameworks.one_line: resting-state timescale aligned with hierarchical position

core_question: How do localized task activations and distributed connectivity changes differ across the cortical hierarchy?
key_assumptions: task activity and FC reflect dissociable processes
principal gradient indexes cortical hierarchical organization
intrinsic timescale and myelin index hierarchical position
manipulations: compared regional task-evoked activity vs task-state FC change
related cortical maps to principal gradient, timescale, and myelin
key_findings: task-evoked activity high in unimodal, low in transmodal areas
task-state FC reduced in unimodal relative to transmodal areas
negative relationship between localized activity and distributed FC across cortex
dissociation aligned with principal gradient, intrinsic timescale, and myelin content
interpretations: cortical hierarchy supports a trade-off between localized and distributed processing
task activity vs FC dissociation indexes macroscale hierarchical organization
